nameMeanings.name: Martel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Andre, Armand, Normand, Marcel, Adrien, Aime, Benoit, Laurent, Yvan, Jacques, Michel.

French and English (Guernsey): nickname for a forceful person or perhaps a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from Old French martel ‘hammer’ (also recorded in late Middle English), from Late Latin martellus. The reference may be to the martel de fer, the iron hammer or mace of medieval warfare, as had been the case with Charles Martel, the grandfather of Charlemagne, who gained his byname from the force with which he struck down his enemies, the Saracens, in the battle of Tours (Poitiers) in 732.
English (Guernsey): from the Middle English and Old French personal name Martel, a pet form of Martin .

variants.name: Martelle
meanings: French: from the feminine form of   Martel   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Martin
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Martell
meanings: French: rare southern variant of   Martel   (compare 2 below) and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Catalan: nickname for a forceful person or perhaps a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from martell ‘hammer’ (from Late Latin martellus; see also Martel 1).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Martel  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Martel of Ruddington and Flawford was born in 1276 as the son of Adam Martel. He had at least 1 son with Margery Rabacy. He died after 1345, in Ruddington, Nottinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Martel of Ruddington and Flawford est né en 1276, fils de Adam Martel. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Margery Rabacy. Il est décédé après 1345, à Ruddington, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Martel of Ruddington and Flawford wurde 1276, als Sohn von Adam Martel geboren. Er hatte mit Margery Rabacy mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb nach 1345 in Ruddington, Nottinghamshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Martel of Ruddington and Flawford è nato il nel 1276 come figlio di Adam Martel. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Margery Rabacy. Morì dopo il 1345, nel Ruddington, Nottinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Martel of Ruddington and Flawfordは1276年にAdam Martelの息子として生まれた。彼はMargery Rabacyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1345年以後にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアルッドイントンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Martel of Ruddington and Flawford(은)는 1276년 Adam Martel의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Margery Rabacy(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1345년 이후 영국 노틴그함실르 루딩턴에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Martel of Ruddington and Flawford nasceu em 1276 filho de Adam Martel. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Margery Rabacy. Ele faleceu após 1345, em Ruddington, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Martel of Ruddington and Flawford родился в 1276 г. Его отца звали Adam Martel. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Margery Rabacy. Он умер после 1345 г. в местности руддингтон, ноттингхамшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Martel of Ruddington and Flawford nació en 1276 como hijo de Adam Martel. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Margery Rabacy. Murió después de 1345, en Ruddington, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.
